content.problemStatement:
Consider the following 4×44 \times 44×4 pattern:

` 1  2  4  7
 3  5  8 11
 6  9 12 14
10 13 15 16
`You are given an integer NNN. Print the N×NN \times NN×N pattern of the same kind (containing integers 111 through N2N^2N2).
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print N lines; each of them should contain N space-separated integers.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The pattern is generated by starting with the number 1 and incrementing it by the row number plus 1 for each column.
The pattern is generated by starting with the number 1 and incrementing it by the row number plus 1 for each column.

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.expectedOutput: The pattern with 1000 rows and 1000 columns.

solution.approach: To generate the pattern, we start with the number 1 and keep incrementing it. We use a 2D array to store the pattern. For each row, we start with the next available number and keep incrementing it by the row number plus 1 for each column. We use a variable to keep track of the current number.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N^2) — because for each test case, we generate the pattern which takes O(N^2) time and we do this for T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N^2) — because we use a 2D array of size N x N to store the pattern.
